cards.answer:
Apartment / flat. Feminine noun (die). Plural: die Wohnungen (add -en).
Derived from the verb 'wohnen' (to live/reside) — the '-ung' suffix turns a verb into a noun.
Pattern: '-ung' nouns are ALWAYS feminine in German. No exceptions.
cards.note: '-ung = -tion' pattern: Wohnung (dwelling), Übung (exercise), Zeitung (newspaper). All feminine. Wohnung ← wohnen, just like 'habitation' ← 'inhabit'.
cards.example: Seit wann haben Sie diese Wohnung? (How long have you had this apartment?)
cards.application: Essential when flat-hunting in Germany — Wohnungssuche (apartment search), Mietvertrag (rental contract).
cards.cloze: Unsere ___ hat drei Zimmer. (Our ___ has three rooms.)

cards.answer:
To go shopping / to shop. Separable: ein|kaufen.
Present: 'Ich kaufe ein.' Auxiliary: haben. Past participle: eingekauft.
Base verb 'kaufen' = to buy; prefix 'ein-' adds the sense of 'stocking up / shopping for supplies'.
cards.note: 'ein-' = in/into. einkaufen = buy in(to your home). Same family: einladen (invite in), einsteigen (step in). Past participle stays together: eingekauft.
cards.example: Ich gehe gerne einkaufen. (I like going shopping.)
cards.application: Useful at the supermarket and when making plans: 'Ich muss noch einkaufen.' (I still need to go shopping.)
cards.cloze: Wir ___ jeden Samstag im Supermarkt ___. (We ___ at the supermarket every Saturday.)
